sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: President & CEO of Qualcomm Inc. Meets PM
feed.summary: The President & CEO of Qualcomm Inc., Cristiano R. Amon, met with Prime Minister Narendra Modi to discuss collaboration between Qualcomm and Indian innovators/startups in AI and other futuristic technologies. They also focused on working together in the semiconductor and chip manufacturing sectors. PM Modi highlighted India's robust ecosystem of innovators and startups.

feed.title: Smurfit Westrock CFO Ken Bowles Sells 10,000 Shares on NYSE
feed.summary: Smurfit Westrock's CFO, Ken Bowles, sold 10,000 ordinary shares of the company on February 19, 2026, at a price of $51.26 per share on the NYSE. This transaction is considered routine insider activity and does not indicate a strategic change for the packaging company. The sale simply provides transparency about senior executive stock dealings, which investors can monitor as one of many indicators of executive confidence.

feed.title: RBC Cuts Price Target on Sixth Street Specialty Lending to $22 From $24, Keeps Outperform Rating
feed.summary: RBC has lowered its price target for Sixth Street Specialty Lending (TSLX) to $22, down from $24, while maintaining an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes after the company recently reported its Q4 2025 earnings and declared its first-quarter 2026 dividend. Sixth Street Specialty Lending operates as a specialty finance company focused on lending to middle-market companies through various debt instruments.

feed.title: Canopy Growth or Tilray Brands: Which Stock Is More Likely to Be a Millionaire Maker?
feed.summary: The article analyzes whether Canopy Growth (CGC) or Tilray Brands (TLRY) has the potential to be a "millionaire maker" stock in the cannabis market. While both companies are positioned to capitalize on potential growth, the industry faces significant regulatory uncertainty. The author concludes that Tilray Brands is a slightly better bet due to its operational and geographical diversification, but neither stock is likely to turn average investors into millionaires, advising caution for both.

feed.title: Uncrustables goes fridge friendly across full portfolio
feed.summary: Uncrustables announced at the Consumer Analyst Group of New York Conference that its entire product portfolio will become "fridge friendly" by summer 2026, allowing sandwiches to be stored in the refrigerator for up to five days. This change eliminates the thaw time barrier for consumers, offering greater convenience for immediate consumption while products will continue to be sold in the freezer aisle. The company also highlighted two new protein-packed morning varieties that are already fridge friendly and available nationwide.

feed.title: AHA, ACC issue guidelines for pulmonary embolism that affect Boston Scientific, Stryker
feed.summary: New guidelines from the American Heart Association (AHA) and American College of Cardiology (ACC) for pulmonary embolism (PE) could significantly benefit Boston Scientific and Stryker, particularly their mechanical thrombectomy (MT) systems. The updated guidelines, the first since 2011, introduce a new classification scheme for PE severity and assign a higher level of evidence for MT use, maintaining its recommendation for massive and submassive PE. Analysts believe this update, which includes detailed categories for risk assessment and therapeutic decision-making, could boost both companies' market position.

feed.title: Savers Value Village, Inc. (NYSE:SVV) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Savers Value Village, Inc. (NYSE: SVV) announced strong Q4 2025 results, marking its first quarter of year-over-year adjusted EBITDA growth in nearly two years, primarily driven by robust U.S. sales and thrift adoption. The company opened 26 new stores in 2025 and plans 25 more in 2026, predominantly in the U.S., while focusing on innovation and efficiency to expand margins and generate free cash flow. For 2026, Savers Value Village projects net sales between $1.76 billion and $1.79 billion, with adjusted EBITDA of $260 million to $275 million, anticipating continued maturation of new stores to drive future profitability.

feed.title: S&P Global CEO, CFO to join March 3 investor webcast from Orlando
feed.summary: S&P Global's President and CEO, Martina Cheung, and CFO, Eric Aboaf, will participate in Raymond James' 47th Annual Institutional Investors Conference on March 3, 2026, in Orlando, Florida. They are scheduled to speak from 11:00 a.m. to 11:30 a.m. ET in an audio-only webcast fireside chat, with Senior Vice President of Investor Relations, Mark Grant, joining for investor meetings. The webcast replay will be available for 90 days following the event, and additional presentation materials will be posted online.

feed.title: Amplify ETFs for stablecoins, tokenization go live for trading
feed.summary: Amplify has launched two new exchange-traded funds (ETFs), the Amplify Stablecoin Technology ETF (STBQ) and the Amplify Tokenization Technology ETF (TKNQ), on the NYSE Arca exchange. These ETFs invest in companies involved in stablecoins and tokenization, reflecting growing interest and regulatory developments in these areas of digital finance. The move aims to capitalize on regulatory clarity and institutional confidence in stablecoin and tokenization technologies.

feed.title: SSR Mining (SSRM) Hits 15-Year High as Earnings Impress
feed.summary: SSR Mining Inc. (SSRM) stock reached a nearly 15-year high due to impressive earnings performance and the early redemption of senior convertible notes. The company reported a significant swing to a net income of $395.7 million in the last year, with revenues soaring by 64% to $1.6 billion. SSR Mining also provided strong production guidance for the current year while announcing the redemption of $230 million in convertible senior notes.

feed.title: Wells Fargo cuts jobs, closes Rosemont office following $4.2B sale of rail business
feed.summary: Wells Fargo is eliminating 45 jobs, primarily commercial bankers, as it closes its Rosemont office. These job cuts follow the bank's $4.2 billion sale of its rail equipment leasing business to a joint venture in 2025, with layoffs scheduled to begin on March 6. Despite the job reductions, Wells Fargo plans to expand its retail presence in the Chicago area, intending to increase its branches from 19 to at least 30.

feed.title: Safe Pro Awarded Contract to Provide U.S. Government with AI Powered Edge Processing Systems
feed.summary: Safe Pro Group Inc. (NASDAQ: SPAI) has been awarded a $1,000,000 subcontract to supply AI processing systems to the U.S. Government. The development and initial production of these systems were strategically funded by leading U.S. firms including ONDAS Inc. and Unusual Machines Inc. Safe Pro specializes in AI-enabled defense, security, and situational awareness solutions, leveraging proprietary machine learning for applications like rapid explosives threat identification.

feed.title: Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) Releases Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.16 EPS
feed.summary: Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) announced strong fourth-quarter earnings, beating analyst expectations by $0.16 EPS with $2.51 per share. The company reported a record 2025 with $4.96 billion in Adjusted EBITDA and provided an optimistic 2026 guidance of $5.4–$5.6 billion, driven by Permian volume growth and new processing capacity. Despite rising capital intensity, Targa maintains a supportive balance sheet with strong liquidity and intends to continue shareholder returns through buybacks and dividends.

feed.title: News brief: Nation-state hackers active on the global stage
feed.summary: Nation-state hackers are escalating cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure globally, as highlighted by a recent IBM report indicating 70% of 2024 attacks involved such infrastructure. The news brief details several recent incidents, including attacks on the U.S. defense industrial base, newly identified hacking groups exploiting OT environments, a campaign against Singapore's telcos, a breach of Senegal's biometric database, and zero-day exploits in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile. These events underscore the urgent need for robust cybersecurity measures to protect critical systems and safeguard data.

feed.title: BIT Capital GmbH Raises Stock Position in Sharplink Gaming Inc. $SBET
feed.summary: BIT Capital GmbH significantly increased its stake in Sharplink Gaming Inc. ($SBET) by 103.5% during Q3, now owning 1,215,819 shares valued at $20.68 million. The stock has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $27.43, yet it exhibits high volatility and a negative P/E ratio. Other institutional investors have also recently acquired stakes in the company.

feed.title: Caterpillar’s Dual-Pronged Strategy: Cementing Leadership Through Tech and Batteries
feed.summary: Caterpillar is strengthening its position in the mining sector by investing heavily in digitalization and electric mobility. This strategy includes acquiring RPMGlobal for mine planning software and investing in ElevenEs to secure a supply of LFP batteries for heavy industrial equipment. These initiatives aim to meet the growing demand for efficient and zero-emission machinery and position Caterpillar as an integrated systems provider for industrial decarbonization, with 2027 marked as a pivotal year for new battery cell production.

feed.title: MGM Experiences High Bearish Option Activity
feed.summary: MGM Resorts (MGM) is experiencing significant bearish options activity, with a high Put/Call Ratio of 4.64 and increased implied volatility, suggesting negative market sentiment. Despite strong revenue growth, the company faces financial concerns due to high debt levels and a low Altman Z-Score, indicating potential financial distress. Investors are awaiting its April 29th earnings report, which could further impact its stock performance, currently trading near historical valuation highs.

feed.title: People Moves: Lucius DiPhillips, CIO at Adobe
feed.summary: Lucius DiPhillips has been appointed as the new Chief Information Officer (CIO) at Adobe, bringing over 25 years of experience in digital transformation and AI innovation. He will lead Adobe's global Technology Services team, focusing on scaling technology infrastructure and modernizing systems to support the company's growth in AI-powered creativity. DiPhillips previously served as CIO at Airbnb, where he spearheaded efforts in modernizing core platforms and accelerating AI adoption.

feed.title: Analysis of Future Potential in Cannabis Stocks
feed.summary: Cannabis stocks like Tilray and Canopy Growth have underperformed recently but hold significant growth potential, especially with changing U.S. regulations; however, federal illegality and market challenges necessitate cautious investment. Tilray's diversified operations and recent licensing deal with Carlsberg indicate strategic expansion, while analysts maintain cautious ratings due to market volatility and legislative risks. The overarching theme is that while opportunities exist due to evolving regulations and strategic business moves, the sector remains speculative due to federal hurdles and intense competition.

feed.title: Safehold (SAFE) Achieves Analyst Price Target Upgrade by RBC Capital | SAFE Stock News
feed.summary: RBC Capital has reaffirmed its 'Outperform' rating for Safehold (SAFE) and increased its price target from $17.00 to $18.00, indicating a 5.88% rise. This upgrade reflects a positive outlook on the REIT's future performance. Despite varying past analyst ratings, the consensus brokerage recommendation for Safehold is "Outperform," with an average one-year target price of $20.10.

feed.title: Is Rigetti Computing (RGTI) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
feed.summary: This article examines analyst recommendations for Rigetti Computing (RGTI), noting an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.58, leaning towards a "Strong Buy." However, it cautions against relying solely on ABRs due to inherent biases of brokerage firms and suggests validating investment decisions with tools like the Zacks Rank. The Zacks Rank for Rigetti Computing is currently a #3 (Hold), conflicting with the optimistic ABR.

feed.title: Hologic’s VP of marketing on the company’s WNBA foray
feed.summary: Hologic, a diagnostics and medical imaging company, has partnered with WNBA star Erica Wheeler for its "Ultimate Defense" campaign, focusing on boosting Pap and HPV screenings among women to prevent cervical cancer. Michelle Janisch, Hologic's VP of marketing, noted the campaign leverages the growing momentum in women's sports and aims to reach a wider audience with important women's health messages. This initiative follows Hologic's previous collaborations with female celebrities like Mary J. Blige to promote health screenings.

feed.title: TTM Technologies Stock Pops on Earnings Beat: Is the Upside Just Starting?
feed.summary: TTM Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: TTMI) has reported stronger-than-expected earnings and raised its outlook, hinting at stabilizing demand in high-reliability electronics across diverse sectors including defense, AI infrastructure, and 5G networking. Despite the positive news, the stock's valuation remains conservative compared to its peers, suggesting potential for further upside. The company's strategic positioning within critical US capital spending streams and its focus on operational discipline and deleveraging make it an appealing mid-cap opportunity for diversified investors.

feed.title: UBS Raises Price Target for Exelon (EXC) While Maintaining Neutr
feed.summary: UBS has raised its price target for Exelon (EXC) to $51.00 from $48.00, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This increase signifies a 6.25% uplift. The article also provides a summary of recent analyst ratings and the average target price from Wall Street analysts, alongside GuruFocus's estimated GF Value.

feed.title: U.S. civil rights agency sues Coca-Cola distributor after allegedly excluding men from work trip
feed.summary: The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) has sued Coca-Cola Beverages Northeast, a regional distributor, for allegedly discriminating against male employees. The lawsuit claims that male employees "suffered damages" after being excluded from a two-day, women-only networking event. The EEOC asserts that the company violated Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 by denying male employees equal resources and opportunities.

feed.title: Firehouse Subs Reignites Growth Plans in King of Prussia and Willow Grove
feed.summary: Firehouse Subs is planning a significant return to the Philadelphia area, specifically targeting King of Prussia and Willow Grove, with ambitions to open 50 to 80 new locations regionally over the next five to ten years. The sandwich chain, acquired by Restaurant Brands International in 2021, aims to launch the first ten of these new restaurants within three years, seeking franchise partners for various location types. This expansion follows their exit from the market in early 2023 for a systemwide transition, with the company now asserting strengthened support systems for growth.

feed.title: QCR Holdings announces increased dividend
feed.summary: QCR Holdings, Inc. has announced an increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.10 per share, up from $0.06. This decision, payable on April 3, 2026, reflects the company's strong financial performance and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. QCR Holdings, the parent company of Quad City Bank & Trust, had $9.6 billion in assets as of December 31, 2025.

feed.title: Sonos Q1/2026 Shows Little Progress; CEO Gives Clues to His Turnaround Plan
feed.summary: Sonos, Inc. reported its Q1 2026 financial results, revealing a slight revenue decline but a significant increase in profits due to aggressive cost-cutting measures, including layoffs and reduced R&D and marketing spending. CEO Tom Conrad outlined a five-point turnaround strategy focusing on product innovation, customer advocacy, intentional marketing, geo-expansion, and AI integration, though the article questions the feasibility and clarity of some aspects. The company's immediate future remains uncertain, with guidance for Q2 2026 suggesting flat revenue growth.

feed.title: SolarEdge Stock Is Surging: What's Going On Today?
feed.summary: SolarEdge Technologies (NASDAQ:SEDG) stock is surging today, outpacing the broader tech sector, driven by strong technical signals and bullish sentiment after the Supreme Court struck down former President Donald Trump's global tariff program. The removal of tariff uncertainty is expected to ease cost pressures for the Israeli-based company, which has global supply chain exposure. Analysts currently maintain a "Hold" rating on SEDG with recent target adjustments from Goldman Sachs, RBC Capital, and Barclays.

feed.title: CMS Energy and Consumers Energy Announces Board Changes, Effective February 20, 2026
feed.summary: CMS Energy and Consumers Energy have announced the appointment of two new members to their boards of directors, effective February 20, 2026. Diane Leopold, former executive vice president and chief operating officer at Dominion Energy, brings over three decades of utility experience and will serve on the Compensation and Human Resources Committee and the Finance Committee. Richard Keyes, president and CEO of Meijer, Inc., also joins the boards, bringing 35 years of operational and strategic experience, and will serve on the Audit Committee and Governance, Sustainability and Public Responsibility Committee.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of PayPal Holdings, Inc. Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against PayPal Holdings, Inc. on behalf of investors who purchased securities between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal created a false impression of reliable revenue outlook and growth, while minimizing risks, leading to a significant stock drop after disappointing Q4 2025 financial results and the withdrawal of 2027 financial targets. Investors have until April 20, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Fridge-friendly Uncrustables are coming to Kroger, other stores
feed.summary: The J.M. Smucker Co. announced that its Uncrustables will soon be "fridge-friendly," allowing consumers to eat them straight from the refrigerator without thawing. This new option will be available at Kroger, Walmart, and Target stores nationwide, with a full rollout expected by summer 2026. Two morning flavors, Up & Apple and Bright‑Eyed Berry, are already on shelves.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Varonis Systems, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Varonis Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: VRNS) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between February 4, 2025, and October 28, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Varonis made materially false statements regarding its ability to convert existing customers to its SaaS offering, leading to artificially inflated stock prices. Investors are encouraged to join the case by March 9, 2026, to potentially recover damages.

feed.title: Is PrimeEnergy Resources (PNRG) Among the Energy Stocks that Lost This Week?
feed.summary: PrimeEnergy Resources (PNRG) stock experienced an 11.92% drop between February 11 and February 18, 2026, placing it among the week's biggest energy stock losers. The article highlights recent insider sales, with Director Clint Hurt and Robert de Rothschild divesting significant shares. Despite its potential, the author suggests certain AI stocks might offer better upside with less risk.

feed.title: Contact Levi & Korsinsky by April 3, 2026 Deadline to Join Class Action Against Picard Medical, Inc.(PMI)
feed.summary: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP has filed a class action lawsuit against Picard Medical, Inc. (PMI) on behalf of investors who suffered losses due to alleged securities fraud between September 2 and October 31, 2025. The lawsuit claims Picard Medical was involved in a fraudulent stock promotion scheme, with insiders using offshore accounts to dump shares. Investors have until April 3, 2026, to request to be appointed as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of PayPal Holdings, Inc. Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against PayPal Holdings, Inc. on behalf of investors who purchased securities between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal created a false impression regarding its revenue outlook and growth projections, minimizing risks and that its 2027 financial targets were unrealistic. This action comes after PayPal disclosed disappointing Q4 2025 earnings and withdrew its 2027 financial targets, leading to a significant drop in its stock price.

feed.title: Park Hotels & Resorts Inc (PK) Misses Q4 2025 Earnings Estimates — EPS $-1.04 vs $0.06 Expected
feed.summary: Park Hotels & Resorts Inc (PK) reported a significant miss on its Q4 2025 earnings, with an EPS of $-1.04 against an expected $0.06, representing a 1792.4% miss. The company's revenue for the quarter was $2.5 billion. Following the announcement, shares declined by 1.4% in after-hours trading to $11.23.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges NuScale Power Corporation Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against NuScale Power Corporation (NYSE: SMR) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between May 13, 2025, and November 10, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that NuScale made false or misleading statements regarding its commercialization strategy and its partner ENTRA1's experience in nuclear power generation. Investors are encouraged to join the case by April 20, 2026, to potentially recover damages.

feed.title: Upstart Announces Inaugural $200M Upstart Auto Forward-Flow Agreement with Wafra
feed.summary: Upstart, an AI lending marketplace, has announced a $200 million forward-flow commitment from Wafra, a global alternative investment manager. This agreement, which is Wafra's first with Upstart, involves the purchase of assets originated through Upstart's auto finance platform over a 12-month period. The partnership aims to provide consistent auto funding, helping Upstart offer competitive rates and processes for borrowers' auto financing needs.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: Starfighters Space Celebrates NYSE American Listing
feed.summary: The NYSE provides a daily pre-market update, highlighting market insights before trading begins. Ashley Mastronardi delivers today's update, focusing on the December PCE Report and Herbalife's latest earnings, which include a new investment from Cristiano Ronaldo. Additionally, commercial space company Starfighters Space will ring the Opening Bell to celebrate its NYSE American listing, with the third annual Space Summit also scheduled.

feed.title: Enphase Energy, Inc. Notice of April 20, 2026 Application Deadline for Class Action Lawsuit - Contact Lewis Kahn, Esq. at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC, Before Application Deadline
feed.summary: Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC (KSF) has announced a class action securities lawsuit against Enphase Energy, Inc. (NasdaqGM: ENPH). The lawsuit, with an application deadline of April 20, 2026, seeks to recover losses for investors affected by alleged securities fraud between April 22, 2025, and October 28, 2025. KSF claims Enphase overstated its ability to manage inventory, offset impacts from the Residential Clean Energy Credit termination, and mispresented its financial and operational prospects.

feed.title: Peoples Bancorp Announces Cash Dividend
feed.summary: Peoples Bancorp of North Carolina, Inc. announced a regular cash dividend of $0.21 per share for the first quarter of 2026. The dividend will be paid on March 16, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 4, 2026. Shareholders are encouraged to enroll in the Company's Dividend Reinvestment and Stock Purchase Plan.

feed.title: Philanthropic partnership backs country-led research to guide the use of AI in health
feed.summary: The Gates Foundation, Novo Nordisk Foundation, and Wellcome have jointly invested US$60 million in the Evidence for AI in Health (EVAH) initiative. This partnership aims to support country-led evaluations of AI health tools in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) to determine their effectiveness and responsible implementation. The first call for proposals focuses on AI-enabled decision support tools for frontline health workers in primary and community settings across sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and Southeast Asia.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum Stock Slips Friday: What's Going On?
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum's stock slipped on Friday despite reporting strong fourth-quarter earnings, beating analyst estimates, and increasing its dividend following significant debt reduction. The company's financial position has improved, and it's focusing on core oil and gas operations, particularly in the Permian Basin, with Berkshire Hathaway as a major stakeholder. Technical indicators show the stock is in overbought territory but maintains bullish momentum, trading above key moving averages.

feed.title: Texas Roadhouse, Inc. (NASDAQ:TXRH) Plans Dividend Increase - $0.75 Per Share
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse (NASDAQ:TXRH) has announced a quarterly dividend increase to $0.75 per share, representing a 10.3% rise, payable on March 31st. This dividend is well-covered by earnings with a payout ratio of 35.8% and expectations for EPS to further improve coverage next year. Despite missing Q4 EPS and revenue estimates, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $197.32.

feed.title: Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation (NYSE:VAC) Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.80
feed.summary: Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation (NYSE:VAC) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.80 per share, payable on March 18th to shareholders of record on March 4th, representing an annualized yield of 5.5%. The dividend is considered well-covered with a historical payout ratio of 41.8% and a projected future payout ratio of 42.8%, based on next year's EPS estimates. The stock currently holds a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with an average price target of $61.13.

feed.title: QCR Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:QCRH) Raises Dividend to $0.10 Per Share
feed.summary: QCR Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:QCRH) announced a 66.7% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.10 per share, payable on April 3rd to shareholders of record on March 19th. This raise reflects the bank's strong financial health, with a low payout ratio of 3.4% and recent earnings per share of $2.21, significantly beating consensus estimates. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $102.25 for the stock.

feed.title: Digital Realty Trust, Inc. (NYSE:DLR) Announces Quarterly Dividend of $1.22
feed.summary: Digital Realty Trust (NYSE:DLR) has announced a quarterly dividend of $1.22 per share, payable on March 31st to shareholders of record by March 13th, representing a 2.8% yield. Despite a current payout ratio of 305%, analysts project the company will cover its dividend with an expected future payout ratio of 63.8% based on estimated future earnings. Stock recently traded down, and institutional investors have been adjusting their positions, with insider Mary Hogan Preusse selling shares in December.

feed.title: Omnicom Group Inc. (NYSE:OMC) Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.80
feed.summary: Omnicom Group Inc. (NYSE:OMC) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.80 per share, payable on April 9th to shareholders of record by March 11th. The company's dividend payout ratio is 36.0%, and analysts project Omnicom Group will earn $8.95 per share next year, enabling it to continue covering its annual dividend. Omnicom stock recently traded up 2.9% to $83.25, with institutional investors holding 91.97% of the shares.

feed.title: Service Corporation International (NYSE:SCI) Plans Quarterly Dividend of $0.34
feed.summary: Service Corporation International (NYSE:SCI) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.34 per share, an increase of 6.3% from its previous dividend, making it the 15th consecutive year the company has raised its payout. The dividend is payable on March 31st to shareholders of record on March 13th, with an ex-dividend date also on March 13th. Analysts project the dividend to remain well-covered with an expected future payout ratio of around 32.3%.
